titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Kent State Collection
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Susan Grigg and staff of Manuscripts and Archives; Quin DeLaRosa, 2025; Janet Lopes, 2025.

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.
This collection contains 35 published digital objects. And those objects are comprised of 66 digital files.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday October 8, 2025 at 03:04
Revised by Janet Lopes to reflect description of July 2025 acquisition.

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, manuscripts drafts, legal papers, clippings, issues of the Daily Kent Stater, interview transcripts, photographs and other material collected by Peter Davies, Paul Keane, Bill Gordon, James Munves and others concerning the disturbance at Kent State University and the shooting of Kent State students by members of the Ohio National Guard on May 4, 1970. The collection also includes a model of the Kent State campus.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gifts of Peter Davies, Michael Astrue, Lesley Wischmann, Paul Keane, Bill Gordon, James Munves, and others, 1976-1994, 1997. Gift of Paul Keane, 2009, 2010, 2015, 2020-2021, and 2023. Gift of Lesley Wischmann, 2025.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Series I, Box 25 is closed due to court order. Researchers must request cotton gloves from the Reading Room Service Desk before handling photographic material in Accession 1989-M-048. Researchers must have written permission from Bill Gordon to use his correspondence in Series III, Box 4. Access to materials in Series II, as well as materials in Accessions 1982-M-016, 1990-M-047, and 2006-M-115 requires the permission of Paul Keane until May 4, 2020. Access to the model of the Kent State site in Series V requires permission in advance from the Director of Manuscripts and Archives. The model is stored at the off-site Library Shelving Facility in five boxes and measures 50 cubic feet when assembled. Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site. Copies of commercially produced audiovisual materials contained in this collection cannot be made for researcher use outside of the repository.
Box 226-227 (audiocassettes and phonographs): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 228 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright is retained by Paul D. Keane for materials in this collection he has authored or otherwise produced. After the lifetime of Paul D. Keane, his copyright becomes the property of his heirs, successors or assigns. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accruals.head: Accruals
accruals.p: This Kent State Collection is closed for additions of new materials except from those whose papers are already included in the collection. The one exception to this is a promised donation of Sanford J. Rosen's files documenting the civil suit brought by the victims' families against Ohio government officials in United States District Court.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Kent State Collection (MS 804). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: ACLU of Ohio Kent State Project Records (MS 1800). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: Notebooks of Peter Davies are available on microfilm (2,000 frames on 2 reels, 16mm.) from Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library, at cost. Order no. HM119.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized in six series and nineteen additions: I. Peter Davies. II. Paul Keane. III. Bill Gordon. IV. James Munves. V. Additional Donors. VI. Lesley Wischmann.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Kent State Collection in Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library, originated with the efforts of Peter Davies, author of The Truth about Kent State , and his friend Paul Keane to find a safe repository for the material that Mr. Davies had created and collected in the course of his research. The major part of the the papers came to Yale on the seventh anniversary of the May 4, 1970, killings, and the Yale Political Union commemorated that gift by sponsoring a five-day colloquium featuring such civil libertarians as Henry Steele Commager and Aryeh Neier as well as Davies, Keane, and others who had long been studying and protesting what happened at Kent State. Since then the library has endeavored to build on this foundation by collecting other relevant manuscripts, but Davies' papers remain the core of the Collection.

The largest quantity of unique material in the Kent State Collection is Peter Davies' correspondence, beginning with his protest to President Nixon on May 8 and spanning seven strenuous years of inquiry, agitation, and organization. This is, above all, a record of the impact of Kent State on Davies and his correspondents: the parents of the victims, the public officials from whom he demanded an accounting, and the citizens who shared his outrage and collaborated in his protests and probings. The letters document in extraordinary detail the development of Davies' understanding of what happened at Kent State and the progress of his collaborative efforts to obtain redress through public opinion and the legal system. In addition, there are notes, drafts, and unpublished writings that add considerably to the public record of his analysis.

The Kent State Collection also contains a large quantity of research material that would be very useful in a fresh inquiry into Kent State and its aftermath. These are the major kinds of material: two feet of newspaper clippings, spanning from 1970 to 1978; several thousand pages of photostatic copies of F. B. I. Documents, purchased by Yale under the Freedom of Information Act; many publication from Kent State, including newspapers, yearbooks, leaflets, and commemorative booklets; and briefs, transcripts, and other documents related to various legal proceedings. These materials thoroughly document the public perception of Kent State, and give easy access to much information that is otherwise available only in widely scattered sources.
Adapted from The Left Review, "Kent State: Ten Years After," 1980, page 75.

The Lesley Wischmann papers contains research material gathered in the 1970s and well into the 2000s during her work with Peter Davies. It includes printouts of extracted FBI files and a comprehensive lead up to the shootings and aftermath, in "Kent diaries for Daily Kos" entries in the Wischmann electronic files. Wischmann also gathered large amounts of research related to Terry Norman, a controversial figure in the Kent State University shootings. Contents of the papers include correspondence with Peter Davies, Elaine Holstein (mother of Jeff Miller, killed at Kent State), and Laurel Krause (sister of Allison Krause, also killed at Kent State).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access Restriction: Trial-relted material in Series I, Box 25 is closed by court order.

accessrestrict.p: These records are closed by court order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to materials in this series requires the permission of Paul Keane until May 4, 2020.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Identified by Keane as the key to his papers and recommended as such to all researchers.

Articles and documents resulting from petition drive

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A significant amount of the correspondence is between Paul Keane and Dr. Glenn A. Olds, president of Kent State University.

accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Morning Exchange" (1973 Sep 17); NBC News (1973 Sep 20); NBC "Scene on Sunday" (1973 Sep 23); and Harry Reasoner report on "Week in Review" (1973 Sep 23).

did.unittitle: Bill Moyers Journal, "Justice Delayed," and Walter Cronkite Show/Charles Kuralt
John Adams, Board of Church & Soc., United Methodist Ch.
Burton Ascheim, friend of Allison Krause's sister, who overheard Judge Young in elevator during Kr. V. Rh.
Alan and Albert Canfora: Alan was one of 9 wounded students; Albert is his father
Ramsey Clark, slated to serve as chief trial counsel for plaintiffs in Kr v Rh until he withdrew from case
John Cleary, one of the 9 wounded students
Peter Davies, associated with Krause & Adams in effort to obtain federal grand jury, author of K. A. Challenge to the Amer. Conscience
Paul Donner, civil libertarian, file contains articles on spying at KSU
David Engdahl, attorney associated with Steven Sindell and later representing Krause in Kr v Rh; law professor at Univ of Colorado, student of martial law, etc.
Marcus Friedman, Dayton attorney who knew Judge Don Young when he was an attorney
Burt Fulton, chief counsel for defendants in Kr v Rh (with C. Brown)
Michael Geltner, ACLU attorney in Kr v Rh
Thomas Grace and parents; Tom was one of 9 wounded students
Fred Hogle, Ravenna att'y, took part in defense of "Kent 25" indicted by special grand jury
Dean Kahler and parents; Dean was one of the 9 wounded students
Nelson Karl, Cleveland ACLU att'y in Kr v Rh (2 folders labelled)
Joseph Kelner, NYC trial lawyer, co-author of The Kent State Coverup, chief counsel for plaintiffs in Kr v Oh 1st trial, 1975
Robert Kelner, NYC trial lawyer, son of Joseph, counsel in Kr v Rh, 1st trial
Arthur Krause, father of Allison Krause, killed at KSU
Prof. Jerry Lewis, KSU Sociology Dept., present at shootings; wrote papers on shootings
Joseph Lewis, Galen Keller: Lewis was one of 9 wounded students, Galen Keller was a para-legal for plaintiffs in Kr v Rh
Mr. and Mrs. Louis Schroeder, parents of Wm Schroeder, killed at KSU
Schroeder interview (belongs with previous interview, this folder had been received in an earlier accession)
Donald Scott Mackenzie, one of the 9 wounded students
Fred Mandel, Cleveland att'y, counsel for plaintiffs in Kr v Rh, 1st trial
Bernard Mazel, direct mail expert who solicited funds for plaintiffs in Kr v Rh; it contains newspaper advertisement and direct mail pieces, as well as program of Artists for Justice benefit.
Mrs. Elaine Holstein, mother of Jeffrey Miller
Sanford Rosen, ACLU att'y, handled appeal in Kr v Rh to 6th circuit and became chief counself for plaintiffs in 2nd trial
Mr. and Mrs. Martin Scheuer, parents of Sandy Scheuer, killed at KSU
David Scribner, NYC att'y, master-minded defense of 'Kent State 25' and won suit to have spec. grand jury report expunged
Robert Stamps and parents; Stamps was one of the 9 wounded students
Benson Wolman, head of Ohio branch of ACLU
Doug Wrentmore, Jan Wrentmore; Doug was one of the 9 wounded students; Jan is his mother
Cases under 1983, civil section of fed civil rts act
Cases under 242, criminal section of fed civil rts act
Complaints in Kr v Rh, these are documents that started civil law suit
Criminal trial, federal US v Shafer
Depositions taken in connection with civil suit (Kr v Rh) summary of
Gag rule of Judge Young, also contains documents relative to Rosen's attempt to overturn rule
Hammond v Brown, civil suit brought by Scribner to overturn report of special grand jury (Brown was Ohio att'y general)
Jencks Act, instrumental in rendering access to Justice Dept. and other investigatory materials, grand jury reports, etc, made available to defense in Kr v Rh
Justice Dept., mostly on efforts to obtain federal grand jury
Kr v Rh appeal to US Supreme Court in 1974; at which plaintiffs obtained right to have civil trial
Ex-parte Milligam, landmark martial law case
Monroe v Paper, landmark case under civil section fed civ rts act (1983) on right to sue law enforcement officers
Morgan v Hevth, civil suit by ACLU against illegal search of KSU dormitories following shootings
Rhodes, legality of order sending ONG to Kent, paper by Engdahl
Schroder v Richardson, attempt to force convening of fed grand jury (Richardson was A.G., US)
Summation of Joseph Kelner in Kr v Rh
Tape and photo index, Kr v Rh
Trial (Kr v Rh, 1975) index to transcript
Defendants Canterbury, Rhodes; Witnesses Delaney, Montgomery

Notebook with tan cover marked K.S. #2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Notebook is paged and has table of contents inside front cover; among other items it contains lists of the Kent State cases (civil and criminal cases arising out of the shootings) and of other legal cases concerning issues raised by KS shootings. It also contains day-by-day chronology of events related to the shootings, from January 1, 1970 to October 30, 1975.

did.unittitle: Notebook with yellow cover, marked K.S. #2

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notes of interview with Steven A. Sindell (SAS), lawyer who represented many of the KS plaintiffs; also notes on Judge Battisti, Tom Grace, Benson Wolman, Steven Keller (para-legal), interview with Chris Jindra (Cleveland Plain Dealer Reporter) (all but Battisti based on interviews).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains documents relating to attorneys' dispute over Judge Thomas' award of legal fees and expenses as part of out of court settlement of Krause v Rhodes.

did.unittitle: Notes on plaintiffs and defendants in Krause v Rhodes
Miscellaneous Kent State material: copies of documents in evidence at trial
Reprint from Cleveland ; biography of Benson Wolman (ACLU)

accessrestrict.p: Access to the model itself requires permission in advance from the Director of Manuscripts and Archives. The model is stored at the off-site Library Shelving Facility in five boxes and measures 50 cubic feet when assembled.

did.unittitle: Voir dire of the Jury-Krause vs. Rhodes
Voir Dire of the Jury-Krause vs. Rhodes pp. 165B-165T?
Deposition of Edward King-Krause vs. Rhodes
Joseph Kelner Kent State Summation (3 tapes)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The accession consists of the records of David E. Engdahl, attorney on the legal team for plaintiffs in the civil suits that arose from the shooting incidents at Kent State University, May 4, 1970. The bulk of the records pertain to Krause et al. v. Rhodes et al. and related cases, which were in litigation from 1970 to 1981. The case was tried twice, first in 1975 and finally in 1978. A settlement was reached in 1979, but there were subsequent appeals related to the payment of attorney's fees and the protective order issued in 1976. These appeals were tried in 1981. In addition to motions, orders, and correspondence with the court, the records include correspondence among counsel, news clippings, notes, and background materials. Engdahl collected and attempted to publish poems commemorating the deaths at Kent State, which are included in the accession.

Of particular interest to researchers are the extensive photographic documentation that Engdahl compiled on behalf of the plaintiffs and the testimony, memoranda, and motions related to the use of photographs as evidence. Several photographers served as witnesses in the trials, and a great deal of testimony was oriented around photographs and maps.
The accession is arranged under seven headings: Legal, Background Materials, General Correspondence, Clippings, Photographs, Audiotapes, and Poetry.

The Legal files are mainly related to Krause et al. v. Rhodes et al., but contain records for other cases directly related or topically relevant to the events at Kent State University. Records pertaining to Krause et al. v. Rhodes et al. are organized in chronological order. Other cases are organized in rough chronological order.

The Background Materials are arranged alphabetically in six sections. Biographical background materials contain notes, clippings and other materials about defendants, witnesses, and jurors collected by Engdahl in preparation for trials. Reports contain background materials prepared by agencies, expert witnesses or individuals and presented as such. Topical background files contain notes, clippings, and other materials organized alphabetically by topic. The witness testimony index is a collation of testimonies, depositions, interviews, and statements from various sources. Writings are mostly Engdahl's, but also contain drafts from other writers.

General Correspondence is organized alphabetically by correspondent and topic. Much of this correspondence relates to legal strategy, relations among plaintiffs and counsel, and public relations surrounding the trials. Correspondence with the court is generally filed with legal papers. Most correspondence regarding poetry and photography is filed with those materials rather than in general correspondence.
Clippings are organized topically and then chronologically.

Photographs are organized by format. Engdahl gathered reprints from many photographers who witnessed the events of May 4. He collated these into the plaintiffs' exhibits, juxtaposed to photographer perspective maps. Engdahl also assembled and organized the photographs in sequence to show the chronology of events on May 4 (boxes 51-52). Many of these photographs are published elsewhere, but the collection contains a significant number of photographs that have not been widely distributed.

scopecontent.p: Many of these photographs contain Plaintiff's Exhibit labels with numbers, and several are identified as being taken by Howard E. Ruffner.

Negatives, 4x5, black and white, some color

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images and proof sheets by the following photographers: Marcia L. Adams, Richard Boeme, Lloyd D. Heilman, Lori Legge, Gregory Moore, Terrance B. Norman, Timothy Oleski, James Peck, Harold Scoobie, Victor Sloan, Patricia Sorse, and Terry Wilson.

Slides documenting the site of the Kent State shootings
Negatives, 35 mm, black and white, of mounted photographs of events at Kent State University on 4 May 1970
Negatives, 8 mm, black and white, of Kent State scenes, including what appear to be bullet holes in buildings

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: As a preservation measure, original materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be used. Digital access copies must be provided for use. Contact Manuscripts and Archives at beinecke.library@yale.edu to request access.

odd.p: See also Series II. Paul Keane Papers and Accessions 1982-M-016 and 2006-M-115.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sanford Rosen, one of the lawyers for the families of Kent State shooting victims, petitioned under the Freedom of Information Act to have materials released pertaining to the United States Department of Justice, Civil Rights Division's investigation of the Kent State killings. The materials released comprise this accession and are arranged in the order in which they were received.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: See also Series II. Paul Keane Papers and Accessions 1982-M-016 and 1990-M-047.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to materials in this accession requires the permission of Paul Keane until May 4, 2020.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Keane's reminiscences and numerous photocopies of material from his journals and diaries relating to the May 4, 1970, shootings at Kent State University.

did.unittitle: Typed and unedited transcription of Keane's journals relating to the Kent State shootings
"Reflections on 'The University and the Community of Learning,'" and text of the speech by Henry Steele Commager on which it is based

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Commager's speech was given at Kent State University on 10 Apr 1971. Keane's reflections are printed from a web site.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

Original audiovisual materials, as well as preservation and duplicating masters, may not be played. Researchers must consult use copies, or if none exist must pay for a use copy, which is retained by the repository. Researchers wishing to obtain an additional copy for their personal use should consult Copying Services information on the Manuscripts and Archives web site.

did.unittitle: "Whiting-Out History," Paul Keane's reflection on the forty-fifth anniversary of the Kent State shootings on May 4, 1970

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes typescript, published version from White River Junction's Valley News , 2015 April 26, and e-mail correspondence relating to the article.

did.unittitle: Concerning petitions: includes Wright-Board of Elections, Sindell, Gonef-Registrar, Jim Bruss, Dr. Bigelow, and Robin Grimmin
Confusion around Ziegler's statements, phone conversation with reporter, Ziegler's secretary, Jerry Warren
Discussion with newsmen and press coverage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of material on the Doonesbury comic strip about the events at Kent State University and the controversy that accompanied the publication of the strip. Materials include a scanned image of the comic by Garry Trudeau attached to a draft of a page from Kent State: A Ten Year Ministry , a scanned image of the November 6, 1972 article on the controversy from the Akron Beacon Journal , and a printout of the online copy of an article written by Paul Keane titled "Forum: One man's role in '72 Doonesbury flap," published in the New Haven Register . Materials are dated to 1972 and 2016.

did.unittitle: Materials on the Doonesbury comic strip controversy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of digital material created or compiled by Paul Keane. The records illustrate Keane's continued engagement with the legacies of the May 4, 1970 shooting at Kent State University through survivor advocacy, commemoration, and personal remembrance.

Content includes various writings by Keane, featuring tributes to Alan Canfora, correspondence with a publishing company, opinion pieces published on Medium.com , and thoughts on topics both relating to and beyond the Kent State shooting. Publication information for the opinion pieces authored by Keane is as follows: "Doonesbury Once," 2020 December 1; "They Took His Legs at Kent State but Not His Heart," 2020 December 2; "Offer of Mercy Tested Church, State and Family," 2020 December 3; "God Choreographs Human Sacrifice," 2020 December 4; "Forgetting Four Dead in Ohio," 2020 December 5.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Computer files in this acquisition were accessioned by the Yale University Digital Accessioning Support Service (DASS). Born digital archival material acquired on media by Yale University Library repositories is accessioned through the DASS prior to researcher use. For more information on the DASS and the actions taken during its accessioning work, click here: Born Digital @ Yale: Digital Accessioning Support Service [https://guides.library.yale.edu/c.php?g=300384&p=3593184].

did.unittitle: "Old Hippies Never Die" and other writings by Paul Keane

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Five Medium.com articles by Paul Keane

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Tributes to Kent State shooting survivor Alan Canfora

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This accession consists of digital material created or compiled by Paul Keane. The records illustrate Keane's continued engagement with the legacies of the May 4, 1970 shooting at Kent State University through survivor advocacy, commemoration, and personal remembrance.

Content includes copies of Keane's 1972 FBI file as well as email correspondence and opinion pieces of Keane's. Correspondence relates to E.M. Schorb's poem "Dirge for the Dead Students" and novel A Portable Chaos . It also includes Keane's proposal for a "May 4th Scholar in Residence" at Kent State University. Publication information for the opinion pieces authored by Keane is as follows: "It's Time for Yale's Kent State Collection To Go To Kent State," New Haven Register , 2021 June 13; "It Will Take More Than a Pothole to Stop Him," Cleveland Plain Dealer , 2021 August 6; "The Powerful Censorship of a Parent Wielding a Five-Letter Word," Cleveland.com , 2021 November 12; "Over J. Edgar's Dead Body - The 1970 Kent State Shootings and the FBI," Cleveland.com , 2022 May 4; "Why Yale Preserves My 1972 FBI File," CT Post , 2022 June 13.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Computer files in this acquisition were accessioned by the Yale University Digital Accessioning Support Service (DASS). Born digital archival material acquired on media by Yale University Library repositories is accessioned through the DASS prior to researcher use. For more information on the DASS and the actions taken during its accessioning work, click here: Born Digital @ Yale: Digital Accessioning Support Service [https://guides.library.yale.edu/c.php?g=300384&p=3593184].

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Ledger with cancelled checks and bank statements from Kent State F.A.C.T.
"Relieving Suicidal Thoughts, and How I Found Help," article by Paul Keane in the Cleveland Plain Dealer
Correspondence between Paul Keane and the Beinecke Library with newspaper clippings

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are arranged in six groupings: research and notes, writings and essays, correspondence, photographic prints, scrapbooks and clippings, and audiovisual material. Materials within these groupings maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains research, manuscripts, correspondence, photographic prints, news clippings, scrapbooks, volumes, audiovisual material, and born-digital material detailing the events, history, and lives of those impacted by the 1970 Kent State shootings.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 226-227 (audiocassettes and phonographs): Restricted fragile. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 228 (born digital): Restricted fragile. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Conny Wischmann correspondence to her sister Lesley Wischmann.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The "Conny Binders" were compiled by Conny Wischmann and her sister, Lesley Wischmann. They were originally on floppy disks and later printed for Lesley's use. They complement the "Who's Who" files and contain source material related to the Kent State shootings.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Baton Rouge shootings, Newsweek article, typescript
Krause v. Rhodes, sixth circuit appeal

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes "Miami and the Siege of Chicago", "Nixon in the White House", "Elaine on Jeff", radio news coverage, Ilona Toth reference materials, "Selling of the President", "Fire in the Streets", "Coming Apart", "The Other Convention in Chicago", "Friendly Fire", "The Krauses", "Witness to Power", "Allison" poem, notes on Peter, reaction comments, letter to Peter, and "Elaine" list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters between Leslie Wischmann and Sanford Rosen, attorney who led the families' civil suit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and notes on Margie Sajong's research on home of Jeffrey Glenn Miller, student killed in the Kent State shootings.

did.unittitle: Weatherman , selected writings, Harold Jacobs, Ed.
Weatherman , various minor / underground press articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Paul Doscher, a friend of Leslie Wischmann, on Harlan D. Logan, father-in-law of Cathie Wilkerson.

did.unittitle: FBI exhibits, list and witness statements

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes press, "Chapter 3", Jerry Lewis letter and opinion piece, letters to the editor, assorted press pieces, photo of Jeff's house, People Magazine piece, and assorted articles.
